answer_options.B: He realizes that valuing the past and accepting change can coexist.
answer_options.C: He understands that new shoes represent future opportunities.
answer_options.D: He decides that worn objects are more reliable than new ones.

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because Diego shows he still values his old shoes by remembering that "they know every path" and taking "one last look" at them before leaving, while also accepting the new shoes his father offers, demonstrating that cherishing memories and embracing change can happen together. Option C is correct because Diego recognizes that the new shoes are connected to the "next trail" and feel "heavier with possibility," showing he sees them as representing future adventures rather than just replacements.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the story.

Tomás stared at the towering climbing wall in the gym. His knees felt shaky. Last week, he had only made it halfway up before sliding back down. 'Come on, you can try the easy side,' said his friend Diego. Tomás shook his head. He walked to the difficult side—the one with tiny blue rocks that looked like scattered marbles. He chalked his hands and started climbing. His arms burned. Twice he slipped, but he caught himself. When he finally touched the top bell, his whole class cheered. Tomás grinned down at Diego. 'The view is better from up here,' he called.

What does this story show about Tomás? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Tomás is determined to challenge himself even when a task is difficult.
answer_options.B: Tomás ignores his friend because he feels angry.
answer_options.C: Tomás keeps trying even after making mistakes.
answer_options.D: Tomás is the best climber in his class.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because Tomás chooses the difficult climbing wall instead of the easy side his friend suggests, showing he wants to challenge himself. Option C is correct because the story states that Tomás slipped twice but caught himself and continued climbing until he reached the top, showing perseverance. Option B is incorrect because there is no evidence that Tomás is angry; he simply chooses a different path respectfully. Option D is incorrect because the story does not compare Tomás to other climbers or state that he is the best; it only describes his personal success on this one climb.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the fable below.

The Wind and the Sun were arguing about who was stronger. Suddenly, they saw a traveler walking down the road, wearing a heavy coat.

"I will make him take off that coat," said the Wind. "Then everyone will know I am stronger."

The Wind blew as hard as he could. He howled and pushed against the traveler. But the harder the Wind blew, the tighter the traveler wrapped his coat around himself. Soon, the Wind was tired and gave up.

Then the Sun came out from behind the clouds. She shone gently down upon the traveler. The warm rays made the traveler feel comfortable. He sat down under a tree and removed his coat.

"See," said the Sun. "I have won. Gentle warmth was stronger than your cold, hard wind."

Which statements describe the central message of the story or explain how that message is shown through key details? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: Gentleness and kindness can be more powerful than force and strength.
answer_options.B: The traveler removed his coat when the Sun shone gently, showing that warm kindness worked better than the Wind's forceful blowing.
answer_options.C: Travelers should always carry umbrellas to protect themselves from strong winds.
answer_options.D: The strongest person always wins by pushing harder than everyone else.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it states the central moral of the fable: that gentle persuasion is more effective than force. Option B is correct because it identifies key details from the text (the Sun's gentle warmth causing the coat removal versus the Wind's force causing the traveler to hold on tighter) and explains how those specific details convey the central message. Option C is incorrect because it offers practical advice about umbrellas that is unrelated to the moral lesson about kindness versus force. Option D is incorrect because it states the opposite of the story's lesson; the text explicitly shows that the Wind's pushing failed while the Sun's gentleness succeeded, proving that pushing harder does not guarantee winning.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the story below.

The North Wind and the Sun once had a dispute about which of them was stronger. They decided to settle the matter by seeing who could first make a traveler remove his thick cloak.

The North Wind began. He blew with all his might, sending freezing gusts that whipped at the traveler. But the harder the wind blew, the more the traveler shivered and pulled his cloak tightly around his body to keep warm.

Next, the Sun tried her approach. She began to shine with gentle, steady warmth. The traveler felt the pleasant heat and loosened his cloak. As the Sun grew brighter and warmer, the traveler became so comfortable that he removed his cloak entirely and sat down to rest.

Thus, the Sun was declared the winner, proving that gentle persuasion can succeed where force fails.

Which statements correctly identify the central message, lesson, or moral of the story AND/OR explain how it is conveyed through key details? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: The story's lesson is that being kind and gentle works better than using force.
answer_options.B: The detail that the traveler removes his cloak because of the warm sun shows how gentleness succeeds.
answer_options.C: The strongest person always wins every contest.
answer_options.D: People should not wear heavy coats on sunny days.

answer_explanation: Options A and B are correct. Option A correctly identifies the central message or moral: gentleness and persuasion are more effective than force. Option B correctly identifies a key detail from the text that conveys this message—the traveler's action of removing his cloak in response to the sun's warmth demonstrates the success of gentle methods. Option C is incorrect because the North Wind was physically stronger but failed to remove the cloak, showing that strength alone does not guarantee success. Option D is incorrect because it mistakes a literal detail about the traveler's clothing for the story's deeper lesson about kindness and force.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the story below. Which sentences best describe the central message or lesson of this story? Select ALL that apply.

Long ago, there was a small hummingbird who wanted to cross a wide desert to reach the sweet cactus flowers on the other side. The other birds laughed and said, "Your wings are too tiny! You will never make it across." The hummingbird did not listen. Each morning, she flew as far as she could, then rested on a cactus to drink nectar and save her strength. She did this again and again, day after day. Finally, after many weeks, she reached the other side and enjoyed the beautiful flowers. The other birds were amazed and realized they had been wrong to doubt her.

answer_options.A: Persistence and hard work can help you reach difficult goals.
answer_options.B: The hummingbird rested on cacti to drink nectar and save her strength.
answer_options.C: You should not give up even when others say you cannot succeed.
answer_options.D: It is best to listen to experienced friends who warn you about difficult tasks.

answer_explanation: Options A and C correctly identify the central message of the story. Option A is correct because the story shows how the hummingbird's persistent effort and hard work over many weeks allowed her to achieve her goal of crossing the desert. Option C is correct because the story teaches that one should not give up even when others express doubt, as the hummingbird succeeded despite the other birds saying she would fail. Option B is incorrect because, although it accurately describes a key detail from the text, it states what happened in the plot rather than the lesson or moral. Option D is incorrect because it contradicts the story's message; the hummingbird succeeded by persevering despite the warnings from the other birds, showing that one should not simply accept others' doubts as truth.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the story below.

Liam was building a tall block tower in the classroom. When he went to get a drink of water, his classmate Zoe noticed that another student was about to bump into the tower. Zoe quickly moved a chair in front of the blocks to block the path. The other student walked around the chair, and Liam's tower stayed safe. Liam thanked Zoe when he returned.

Based on the story, which sentences describe Zoe or explain how her actions contribute to the sequence of events? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Zoe is careful and looks out for her classmates.
answer_options.B: Zoe moving the chair keeps the tower from falling down.
answer_options.C: Zoe feels jealous of Liam's tall tower.
answer_options.D: Liam bumps into the tower and knocks it over.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it describes Zoe's character trait (careful) and her motivation (looks out for classmates) based on her actions in the story. Option B is correct because it explains how Zoe's specific action (moving the chair) directly contributes to the sequence of events (preventing the tower from falling). Option C is incorrect because the story shows Zoe helping protect the tower, which demonstrates care rather than jealousy. Option D is incorrect because it contradicts the story; Liam does not bump into the tower—another student almost does, and Zoe prevents it.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the story below.

Marcus was determined to win the class recycling challenge to earn a pizza party. He noticed that the bin in the art room was overflowing with paper scraps that could be recycled. Instead of collecting only from his own classroom, Marcus spent his afternoon helping the art teacher sort and clean the papers before adding them to his count. When the principal announced the winner, Marcus had collected the most items, but the art teacher explained that he had broken the rules by adding non-classroom materials, so he could not win the prize. The class decided to share the pizza with Marcus anyway because his mistake had actually helped the school recycle more waste than ever before.

Which sentences explain how Marcus's actions contributed to the sequence of events? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Marcus's desire to win motivated him to collect paper from outside his own classroom.
answer_options.B: Marcus's choice to add art room materials caused him to break the contest rules.
answer_options.C: Marcus's hard work helped the school recycle more waste than ever before.
answer_options.D: Marcus won the pizza party because he collected more items than anyone else.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it connects Marcus's motivation (his determination to win) to his action of gathering extra paper, which directly affects the plot by giving him the highest item count. Option B is correct because it explains the causal relationship between his specific action of including non-classroom materials and the consequence of breaking the contest rules. Option C is correct because it describes how his actions led to the unintended but positive outcome of increasing the school's recycling total. Option D is incorrect because, despite having the most items, Marcus was disqualified for breaking the rules and did not officially win the pizza party; the class voluntarily shared with him afterward.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the story below.

When the class aquarium started leaking during math time, Maya quickly grabbed a bucket while other students stepped back. She remembered how sad she felt when her goldfish died last year. Even though she was worried about getting water on her new shoes, she held the bucket steady until the teacher fixed the leak. The next day, the teacher chose Maya to be the classroom helper because she had shown she could stay calm in an emergency.

Which statements correctly describe Maya's traits, motivations, or feelings AND/OR explain how her actions contribute to the sequence of events? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Maya's action of holding the bucket helps prevent the water from spilling and leads to her becoming classroom helper.
answer_options.B: Maya is careless about her belongings because she lets her shoes get wet.
answer_options.C: Maya is motivated by her memory of losing her goldfish to save the class fish.
answer_options.D: Maya freezes in emergencies and waits for others to solve problems.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it explains how Maya's action of holding the bucket steady contributes to the sequence of events: it prevents water from spilling and results in the teacher choosing her as classroom helper the next day. Option C is correct because it describes Maya's motivation and feelings; remembering her own loss makes her want to protect the class fish. Option B is incorrect because the text states Maya was worried about her shoes getting wet, which shows she cares about her belongings, not that she is careless. Option D is incorrect because Maya acts quickly to grab the bucket and help, rather than freezing or waiting for others.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the sentence below.

After playing soccer in the hot sun for an hour, Marco drank three glasses of water and said, "I was dying of thirst!"

Which statements about the phrase "dying of thirst" are correct? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: The phrase means Marco was extremely thirsty.
answer_options.B: The phrase means Marco was actually dying and needed a doctor.
answer_options.C: The phrase is an example of nonliteral language.
answer_options.D: The phrase describes exactly what happened to Marco.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the context shows Marco drank three glasses of water after exercise, indicating the phrase means he was very thirsty. Option C is correct because "dying of thirst" is an exaggeration used for emphasis rather than a literal statement of fact, making it nonliteral language.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the sentence below.

The autumn wind was a mischievous artist, painting the trees in hues of fire and gold. When Maria heard the disappointing news, her heart sank like a stone into a deep well, and the world seemed to turn gray and silent around her.

Which statements correctly explain the meaning of the nonliteral language used in the sentence? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: The wind is described as a 'mischievous artist' to show it creatively changes leaf colors like a painter making art.
answer_options.B: 'Painting the trees' means the wind was using brushes and actual wet paint on the tree trunks.
answer_options.C: 'Heart sank like a stone' means Maria suddenly felt heavy with sadness and disappointment.
answer_options.D: 'World seemed to turn gray' means the weather outside became cloudy and foggy.

answer_explanation: Options A and C correctly interpret the nonliteral language. In Option A, describing the wind as a 'mischievous artist' is a metaphor comparing the changing of leaf colors to an artist creating a painting. In Option C, 'heart sank like a stone' is a simile meaning Maria felt a sudden, heavy sadness, not that her heart literally dropped. Option B is incorrect because it takes 'painting' literally, suggesting actual paint and brushes rather than the metaphorical changing of leaf colors. Option D is incorrect because it interprets 'turn gray' as a literal weather change rather than a metaphor describing how sadness made everything seem gloomy and colorless to Maria.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the sentence below.

The math test was a breeze for Carlos. He finished all the problems in ten minutes and didn't miss a single question.

Which statements correctly explain the meaning of the phrase **was a breeze** as it is used in the text? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: It means the test was very easy and simple.
answer_options.B: It means there was actual wind blowing through the classroom.
answer_options.C: It is an example of nonliteral language.
answer_options.D: It means the test was difficult and confusing.

answer_explanation: Option A correctly interprets the nonliteral phrase 'was a breeze' to mean that the test was very easy and simple for Carlos. Option C correctly identifies that this phrase is nonliteral (figurative) language, comparing the ease of the test to the lightness of wind, rather than a literal description of weather. Option B is incorrect because it interprets the phrase literally, suggesting actual wind was present in the classroom, which contradicts the context. Option D is incorrect because it states the opposite meaning; the context clues 'finished in ten minutes' and 'didn't miss a single question' indicate the test was easy, not difficult.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the excerpt from the book *Mia's Mystery* below. Select ALL that apply.

**Chapter 1**
Mia found a shiny gold key under the old oak tree in her backyard. She wondered what door it would open.

**Chapter 2**
The next morning, Mia tried the key in the garden shed. It turned with a click! Inside, she found a map to hidden treasure.

Which statements correctly describe the parts of this text?

answer_options.A: The text is divided into sections called chapters.
answer_options.B: The second part builds on the first by showing Mia using the key she found.
answer_options.C: The text is divided into sections called stanzas.
answer_options.D: The text is divided into sections called scenes.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the text is labeled with 'Chapter 1' and 'Chapter 2', which shows it is organized into chapters. Option B is correct because the second chapter continues the story by showing Mia using the key that was introduced in the first chapter, demonstrating how successive parts build on earlier sections.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the excerpt from a chapter book below.

**Chapter 1**
Maya found an old map in her grandmother's attic. The map showed a winding path through the forest behind the house, ending at a large red X. "This must lead to hidden treasure!" Maya whispered, her heart racing. She packed a backpack and decided to follow the path the very next morning.

**Chapter 2**
Maya followed the twisting trail for over an hour, pushing through thick bushes and climbing over fallen logs. When she finally reached the spot marked with the red X, she found only a rusty shovel and a weathered note. She picked up the note and read: "The real treasure is the journey, not the gold." Maya smiled, realizing that the adventure itself had been exciting.

Which statements describe how Chapter 2 builds on Chapter 1? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Chapter 2 shows Maya completing the action she planned at the end of Chapter 1.
answer_options.B: Chapter 2 introduces a new character who helps Maya understand the map.
answer_options.C: Chapter 2 resolves the question about what the red X marks.
answer_options.D: Chapter 2 describes the same events from Chapter 1 but uses different words.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because at the end of Chapter 1, Maya plans to follow the path the next morning, and Chapter 2 shows her actually taking that journey, building directly on the previous action. Option C is correct because Chapter 1 establishes the mystery of what the red X represents (implied treasure), and Chapter 2 reveals what is actually there (the shovel and note), resolving that question from the earlier section. Option B is incorrect because no new characters appear in Chapter 2; Maya is alone throughout both chapters. Option D is incorrect because Chapter 2 describes new events that occur after Chapter 1 (the journey and discovery), rather than repeating the same events from Chapter 1.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the excerpt from the book below.

**Chapter 1**
Maya found a strange gold key under her pillow. It had tiny stars carved into the handle. She tried it on every door in her room, but none of the locks would turn. She wondered if she would ever find what the key was for.

**Chapter 2**
The next morning, Maya tried the key on the old attic door. The lock clicked open! Inside a dusty chest, she discovered letters written by her great-grandmother many years ago.

Which statements correctly describe the structure of this text? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: The text is divided into parts called chapters.
answer_options.B: The second chapter builds on the first chapter by solving the mystery of the key.
answer_options.C: The text is divided into parts called stanzas.
answer_options.D: The events in Chapter 2 happened before the events in Chapter 1.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the text uses the headings 'Chapter 1' and 'Chapter 2' to show it is organized into parts called chapters. Option B is correct because Chapter 1 introduces the mystery of the key (finding it but not knowing what it opens), and Chapter 2 continues the story by showing Maya finding the matching lock and discovering the chest, thereby building on the earlier section to solve the problem. Option C is incorrect because stanzas are groups of lines found in poems, not in stories divided into chapters. Option D is incorrect because Chapter 2 states that the events happen 'the next morning,' showing it occurs after Chapter 1, not before.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the passage below.

The dark clouds rolled in, and soon rain was pouring down. Maya frowned at the window. "This is the worst day ever," she said. "Our picnic is ruined, and now everything will be wet and muddy."

The narrator looked out at the garden. The flowers had been drooping in the heat, and the grass was turning brown. "This rain is exactly what we need," the narrator thought.

Which statements show different points of view about the rain from Maya, the narrator, or a reader? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Maya believes the rainy day is terrible because it ruins her picnic.
answer_options.B: The narrator thinks the rain is good because it helps the garden.
answer_options.C: A reader might disagree with Maya and enjoy playing outside in the rain.
answer_options.D: Both Maya and the narrator are angry about the rain ruining their plans.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it accurately captures Maya's negative point of view, as shown by her calling it the "worst day ever" and complaining about her ruined picnic. Option B is correct because it reflects the narrator's positive point of view, noting that the rain helps the drooping flowers and brown grass. Option C is correct because it represents a reader's own point of view that differs from Maya's, demonstrating how a reader can have a different opinion from a character. Option D is incorrect because only Maya is upset; the narrator is not angry and actually views the rain as beneficial.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the passage below.

The rain hit the windows of the old cabin. "This is the worst vacation ever," groaned Max, burying his face in a pillow. "We're trapped in this dusty, boring shack with nothing to do." Outside, the sky was dark gray. Max sighed, sure that his summer was ruined. He didn't see the deck of cards on the table, the stack of unread adventure books, or his grandmother smiling as she made bread in the cozy kitchen.

Which statements correctly show how your point of view as a reader differs from Max’s point of view or the narrator’s description? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: You might think the cabin sounds cozy and fun, but Max believes it is a terrible place to spend his vacation.
answer_options.B: You and Max both feel that being stuck inside on a rainy day is always boring.
answer_options.C: You can see there are games and books available to enjoy, but Max complains there is nothing to do.
answer_options.D: The narrator describes the cabin as dusty and boring, which matches exactly how you see the cabin too.

answer_explanation: Option A correctly identifies a difference between the reader’s point of view and a character’s point of view: while Max views the cabin as a terrible place, a reader might notice the positive details and see it as cozy and fun. Option C correctly distinguishes the reader’s awareness from Max’s limited perspective by noting that a reader can observe the available activities (cards and books) that the narrator mentions, even though Max claims there is nothing to do.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the passage below.

Rain pounded against the classroom windows. "This is the best day ever!" shouted Jake, pressing his face against the glass. I sank lower in my seat. The thunder rumbled like a hungry stomach, and I pulled my hoodie over my ears. While Jake watched lightning zigzag across the sky, I kept my eyes on my math worksheet, wishing the storm would pass.

Which statements correctly describe a difference between the narrator's point of view and Jake's point of view about the storm? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: The narrator thinks the thunder sounds scary, but Jake is excited by the storm.
answer_options.B: The narrator wants the storm to end, but Jake is happy the storm is happening.
answer_options.C: Both the narrator and Jake enjoy watching the lightning through the window.
answer_options.D: The narrator feels uncomfortable during the storm, while Jake feels joyful.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the narrator describes the thunder as rumbling "like a hungry stomach," suggesting fear or discomfort, while Jake shouts that it is the "best day ever," showing excitement. Option B is correct because the narrator is "wishing the storm would pass," indicating a desire for it to end, whereas Jake's words and actions show he is happy the storm is occurring. Option D is correct because the narrator "sank lower" in the seat and covered their ears, showing discomfort, while Jake is described as joyful with his enthusiastic shouting and pressing against the glass. Option C is incorrect because only Jake enjoys watching the lightning; the narrator keeps their eyes on their worksheet and does not enjoy the storm.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the story excerpt and the description of the illustration. Then answer the question.

**Story:** "Lily walked slowly down the cracked sidewalk. The old house at the end of the road looked empty and silent. She shivered and wrapped her arms around herself as the wind blew."

**Illustration:** The picture shows a dark, cloudy sky above a large, shadowy house with boarded-up windows. Lily is drawn looking small, with her arms tightly wrapped around her shoulders, standing against the gloomy background.

How do specific aspects of the illustration contribute to what the words convey? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: It creates a scary mood by showing the dark clouds and shadowy house.
answer_options.B: It emphasizes that Lily feels frightened by showing her wrapped arms and small size.
answer_options.C: It shows that the house is brand new with bright lights shining inside.
answer_options.D: It highlights the setting as an old, abandoned place by showing the boarded-up windows.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the dark clouds and shadowy house shown in the illustration create a scary or gloomy mood that matches the text’s description of the empty, silent house. Option B is correct because depicting Lily with wrapped arms and looking small visually emphasizes the nervous or frightened feeling described in the text when she shivers. Option D is correct because the boarded-up windows visually establish the setting as old and possibly abandoned, reinforcing the atmosphere conveyed by the words.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the passage and the description of the illustration. Then answer the question.

**Passage:** Marcus walked through the forest, calling out, "I'm not scared at all! This is just an evening stroll!" He laughed loudly and whistled a happy song, swinging his arms as he walked.

**Illustration Description:** The illustration depicts a dark, rainy forest with bare, twisted trees. Marcus is shivering and looking over his shoulder nervously. A distant lantern glows faintly through the downpour, casting an eerie light on the muddy path.

How do specific aspects of the illustration contribute to what is conveyed by the words in the story? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: It shows Marcus's true fear through his shivering and nervous glance.
answer_options.B: It creates a gloomy, frightening mood with the dark rain and twisted trees.
answer_options.C: It contrasts Marcus's happy whistling with the scary forest surroundings.
answer_options.D: It proves the forest is a safe and cheerful place to walk.

answer_explanation: Options A, B, and C are correct. Option A is correct because the illustration emphasizes an aspect of Marcus's character—his fear is visible through his shivering and nervous glance, contradicting his claim of not being scared. Option B is correct because the dark rain, twisted trees, and eerie lantern glow create a gloomy mood and emphasize the frightening setting. Option C is correct because the illustration contributes to the story by contrasting Marcus's words and actions (laughing, whistling) with the visual reality of the scary surroundings. Option D is incorrect because the illustration shows a dark, rainy forest with an eerie light, suggesting danger rather than safety.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the story excerpt and the description of the illustration. Then answer the question.

**Story:** Maya pulled her coat tighter as she left the main road. The wind roared through the tall pines, and cold rain dripped down her neck. Somewhere in the darkness ahead, she knew the old lighthouse keeper's cottage waited with its warm lantern burning.

**Illustration:** The picture shows a dense pine forest at night. Heavy rain streaks down through the darkness, and the tree trunks look thick and shadowy. Far in the distance, a single small yellow light glows faintly through the trees.

How does the illustration contribute to what is conveyed by the words in the story? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: It creates a frightening and lonely mood that matches Maya's difficult journey through the storm.
answer_options.B: It shows that Maya is already inside the warm, dry cottage resting by the fire.
answer_options.C: It emphasizes how dark and rainy the forest setting is, making her walk seem harder.
answer_options.D: It shows that Maya is laughing and enjoying the rainy weather.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the dark, rainy forest illustration creates a scary, gloomy mood that matches the words describing the 'roaring' wind and cold rain, helping readers feel Maya's discomfort. Option C is correct because the visual details of heavy rain and shadowy trees emphasize the difficult setting described in the text, showing why her journey is challenging. Option B is incorrect because the illustration shows the lantern far in the distance, meaning Maya is still outside in the forest, not yet inside the cottage. Option D is incorrect because the text describes Maya pulling her coat tighter against the cold, suggesting she is uncomfortable, not laughing and enjoying the weather.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the summaries of two stories about Leo from the "Leo's Adventures" series by the same author.

**Story 1: Leo and the Park Puppy**
Leo was playing soccer at the park when he found a puppy with no collar. He stayed with the puppy and asked everyone nearby if they had lost a dog. Finally, he found the owner and returned the puppy safely.

**Story 2: Leo and the Library Wallet**
Leo was reading at the town library when he saw a wallet on a chair. He gave it to the librarian, who found the owner's name inside a library card. The owner came back later and thanked Leo for being honest.

Which statements correctly compare or contrast these two stories? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Both stories take place at the same location.
answer_options.B: In both stories, Leo finds something that belongs to someone else.
answer_options.C: Both stories show that being honest and helpful is important.
answer_options.D: In both stories, Leo uses a phone to call the owner.

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because in both stories, Leo discovers an item that is not his—a lost puppy in the park and a lost wallet in the library—and takes action to return it to its owner. Option C is correct because both stories share the theme of honesty and helpfulness, demonstrated by Leo's choice to return the lost property rather than keep it.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the two story summaries below from the Magic Map series by the same author.

**Story 1: The Garden Secret**
Jade finds a glowing map in her grandmother's attic. Following the map's riddles, she digs beneath the old oak tree in the vegetable garden and discovers a chest of enchanted seeds. Using the map's magic and her knowledge of gardening, she grows plants that survive the drought, saving the community garden from failure.

**Story 2: The Mountain Rescue**
During a class hiking trip, Jade brings the same glowing map. When a sudden blizzard traps her group on a snowy ridge, the map reveals the entrance to a hidden cave. Using the map's light and her knowledge of weather safety, she leads her classmates to shelter and helps rescue a lost hiker.

Which statements correctly compare or contrast the two stories? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: Both stories show Jade using a magical object and her own wisdom to solve problems and help others.
answer_options.B: The settings are the same because both stories take place in a garden during dangerous weather.
answer_options.C: In both plots, Jade must locate a hidden spot (under the oak tree and inside the cave) to resolve the danger.
answer_options.D: Only the second story shows Jade caring about helping other people; the first is about a personal treasure hunt.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because both stories demonstrate the theme that Jade combines the magic of the map with her own wisdom—gardening knowledge in the first story and weather safety knowledge in the second—to overcome challenges and help others. Option C is correct because in both plots, Jade must locate a specific hidden place (the chest beneath the oak tree and the cave on the mountain) to successfully resolve the problem. Option B is incorrect because the settings are different: the first story takes place in a vegetable garden at her grandmother's house, while the second takes place on a snowy mountain during a hiking trip. Option D is incorrect because both stories show Jade helping others; in the first story, she saves the community garden, which benefits many people, rather than seeking personal treasure.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the two summaries of stories about Maya written by the same author.

**Story 1: Maya and the Garden Treasure**
Maya loves exploring her grandmother's garden. One rainy afternoon, she finds a muddy key half-buried under the rose bushes. She washes it off and discovers it opens a small wooden chest hidden inside a hollow tree. Inside is a map to a secret picnic spot. Maya follows the map and shares a snack with her grandmother.

**Story 2: Maya and the Beach Mystery**
Maya visits the ocean with her family. While building a sandcastle, her shovel hits something hard. She digs carefully and uncovers a metal box covered in shells. Inside is a message in a bottle from a child who lived long ago. Maya writes a new note, puts it back, and buries the box for someone else to find later.

Which of the following statements correctly compare or contrast the two stories? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Both stories take place in the same setting.
answer_options.B: In both stories, Maya discovers a hidden object by digging or searching.
answer_options.C: The theme of both stories is that curiosity can lead to exciting discoveries.
answer_options.D: In both stories, Maya solves a mystery by reading a map.

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because in the first story Maya discovers a key buried under rose bushes, and in the second she uncovers a metal box while digging in the sand; both plots involve finding hidden objects through active searching. Option C is correct because both stories show Maya exploring her surroundings and finding something special, sharing the theme that curiosity leads to rewarding discoveries. Option A is incorrect because the settings are different: the first story takes place in a garden, while the second takes place at a beach. Option D is incorrect because only the first story involves a map; the second story involves finding a message in a bottle, not solving a mystery with a map.
subject: ELA

question: The third-grade class was assigned to read an article about how different animals build their homes for today's discussion. Which students show that they came to the discussion prepared by using what they read and connecting it to other ideas to explore the topic? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Mia, who read the article about wolves twice and shares a question she wrote down about how wolf packs work together, then mentions she saw a documentary about wolves that helps her understand the article better.
answer_options.B: Jamal, who brings his highlighted copy of the article and points to a specific paragraph about bear hibernation to support his idea about why animals sleep through winter, and then asks the group what they think happens in warm climates.
answer_options.C: Alex, who read the article about wolves carefully before class but sits quietly during the discussion because he is afraid his ideas might be wrong, so he never shares what he learned or asks questions.
answer_options.D: Priya, who did not read the assigned article about animal homes and instead describes a bird nest she saw in her backyard yesterday, never mentioning any facts from the reading.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because Mia demonstrates preparation by reading the article twice and bringing a written question, and she explicitly draws on that preparation along with other information (the documentary) to explore ideas about wolf behavior. Option B is correct because Jamal shows preparation by bringing a highlighted copy and pointing to specific text, and he uses that evidence to explore ideas by asking the group about related concepts in different climates. Option C is incorrect because although Alex read the material (showing preparation), he does not draw on that preparation to explore ideas under discussion; he stays silent and does not contribute. Option D is incorrect because Priya did not read the required material, so she cannot draw on that preparation; she only shares unrelated personal observations.
subject: ELA

question: Ms. Patel asked her class to read an article about how bees communicate before today’s science discussion. During the discussion, she listens for students who came prepared and are using what they read—along with what they already know—to explore new ideas about the topic. Which students meet this goal? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Jada points to her notes about the bee dance and connects it to her dog spinning when excited, then asks if other animals share similar signals.
answer_options.B: Tyler admits he did not read the article and asks a classmate to summarize the main points.
answer_options.C: Marcus reads a quote about the waggle dance from his marked article, connects it to how ants move slower when cold, and wonders if bees dance differently in winter.
answer_options.D: Sofia lists three facts from the article about bee communication but stays silent when asked to explain what the facts mean.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because Jada shows preparation by pointing to notes from the article and draws on other information (her knowledge of her dog’s behavior) to explore ideas by asking about other animal signals. Option C is correct because Marcus demonstrates preparation by reading a specific quote from his annotated text and draws on other information (his knowledge about ants) to explore ideas by wondering how temperature affects bee dancing. Option B is incorrect because Tyler admits he did not read the required material. Option D is incorrect because although Sofia read the article, she only recites facts without connecting them to other knowledge or exploring the ideas further.
subject: ELA

question: The class read an article titled 'Surviving the Desert Heat.' During today's discussion, which students demonstrate that they came prepared by drawing on the article AND connecting it to other information they know about the topic? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Maria says, 'The article explained that camels store fat in their humps. This connects to what I learned last year about how some lizards store fat in their tails.'
answer_options.B: Jamal says, 'I didn't finish reading the article last night, but I can guess that most desert animals probably sleep underground during the day to avoid the heat.'
answer_options.C: Leo says, 'According to the text, desert foxes have large ears to release heat. That is similar to how elephants use their big ears to cool down, which I read about in a different book.'
answer_options.D: Taylor says, 'I remember watching a movie about deserts last summer, and it showed a snake hunting for food at night when the sand was cooler.'

answer_explanation: Options A and C are correct because both students demonstrate they read the required article by explicitly referencing specific details from it (camels storing fat in humps, desert foxes having large ears) and then connect that information to other knowledge they have about the topic (lizards storing fat in tails, elephants using ears to cool down). Option B is incorrect because Jamal admits he did not finish the reading, so he did not come prepared as required. Option D is incorrect because Taylor only references outside information from a movie without drawing on any evidence or ideas from the assigned article, failing to demonstrate preparation for the specific discussion.
subject: ELA

question: During a class discussion about a story they read, students want to make sure they are following the agreed-upon rules. Which actions show that students are following the rules for discussion? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: Waiting for a pause, then raising a hand to share an idea
answer_options.B: Looking at the speaker and paying attention to what they say
answer_options.C: Calling out an answer without waiting to be called on
answer_options.D: Whispering to a neighbor about a different topic while others are talking

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because waiting for a pause and raising a hand demonstrates gaining the floor in a respectful way, which follows the agreed-upon rules. Option B is correct because looking at the speaker and paying attention shows listening to others with care. Option C is incorrect because calling out violates the rule of speaking one at a time and gaining the floor respectfully. Option D is incorrect because whispering about an unrelated topic prevents listening to others with care and breaks the rule of speaking one at a time about the text under discussion.
subject: ELA

question: During a class discussion about a novel, the teacher asks students to share their thoughts on the main character's decision. Which of the following actions show students following the agreed-upon rules for discussion? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Maya raises her hand and waits quietly until the teacher calls her name. When she speaks, she looks at the class and says, 'I think the character was brave because...'
answer_options.B: Leo looks directly at the student who is speaking, nods his head, and waits until she is completely finished before raising his hand to share a related idea.
answer_options.C: Zoe wants to add something important, so she politely says, 'Excuse me,' and begins speaking while another student is still talking about the book. She stays on topic about the character.
answer_options.D: Sam waits for his turn with his hand raised, but when called on, he tells a long story about his own dog instead of discussing the character.

answer_explanation: Options A and B are correct because they demonstrate following agreed-upon discussion rules. Option A shows gaining the floor in respectful ways by raising a hand and waiting to be called on, and speaking about the topic under discussion. Option B shows listening to others with care by looking at the speaker and nodding, and also demonstrates speaking one at a time by waiting until the speaker is finished. Option C is incorrect because although Zoe is polite and on topic, she violates the rule to speak one at a time by interrupting while another student is still talking. Option D is incorrect because although Sam follows the rule for gaining the floor respectfully, he violates the rule to speak about the topics and texts under discussion by talking about his dog instead of the character.
subject: ELA

question: During a class discussion about a book they just read, the students are following agreed-upon rules for respectful conversations. Which of the following actions show students following these rules? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Maya raises her hand and waits until she is called on before sharing her idea about the story.
answer_options.B: Tom looks at the speaker and nods to show he is listening carefully while she talks about the book.
answer_options.C: Lisa starts talking about her weekend plans while the group is discussing the story.
answer_options.D: Jake interrupts his classmate to say he disagrees with what was just said.

answer_explanation: Options A and B are correct because they demonstrate following agreed-upon discussion rules. Option A shows gaining the floor in a respectful way by raising a hand and waiting to be called on, which follows the rule of speaking one at a time. Option B shows listening to others with care through eye contact and nonverbal acknowledgment. Option C is incorrect because Lisa is not staying on the topic under discussion; she is changing the subject to her weekend plans. Option D is incorrect because Jake is interrupting his classmate, which violates the rule of speaking one at a time and prevents careful listening.
subject: ELA

question:
Tom is giving a presentation about how to plant a sunflower seed. He says, "First, you put the seed in a cup with soil. Then you water it and put it near a window where it can get sunlight."

Which responses show that classmates are asking questions to check understanding, staying on topic, and linking their comments to Tom's remarks? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: How often should we water the seed after we plant it?
answer_options.B: I have a cat that likes to sit in the window.
answer_options.C: Do we need to keep the soil wet or just a little damp?
answer_options.D: My mom bought sunflower seeds at the store last week.

answer_explanation: Options A and C are correct. Option A asks a clarifying question about the watering instructions Tom gave, showing the listener wants to check their understanding of that specific step and linking to his remarks. Option C asks a question to clarify how much water is needed, which stays on topic and links directly to Tom's remarks about watering the seed. Option B is incorrect because it changes the topic to a pet cat instead of the presentation about planting seeds. Option D is incorrect because it does not ask a question to check understanding, nor does it link a comment to Tom's specific remarks about the planting process; it simply shares unrelated personal information.
subject: ELA

question:
During a science presentation, Jordan explains the steps of an experiment: "First, we mix the baking soda and vinegar in the bottle. When they touch, a chemical reaction happens that makes carbon dioxide gas. The gas bubbles up and pushes the balloon over the bottle opening, so the balloon inflates."

Which questions show a student asking to check understanding of the information presented, staying on topic, and linking their comments to Jordan's remarks? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: So, is it the gas bubbles that push into the balloon, or does the liquid itself rise up and fill it?
answer_options.B: Does the balloon start to inflate right when the liquids touch, or does it take a few seconds for the gas to push up?
answer_options.C: Can we try mixing the baking soda with other liquids like juice or water to see if they also blow up the balloon?
answer_options.D: Does the chemical reaction make carbon dioxide because baking soda is a base and vinegar is an acid, or is it some other reason?

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it links directly to Jordan's remarks about gas pushing the balloon and asks a clarifying question to check understanding of the mechanism described. Option B is correct because it connects to Jordan's explanation about the timing of the reaction and asks a question to verify understanding of the sequence. Option C is incorrect because it shifts the topic to other possible experiments rather than checking understanding of the specific information Jordan presented. Option D is incorrect because it asks for scientific reasoning beyond the information presented rather than checking understanding of the process Jordan described.
subject: ELA

question:
During a science discussion, Maya presents information about Arctic foxes. She explains that they have thick fur and small ears to stay warm, and that their fur changes from brown to white in winter to hide from predators.

Which questions demonstrate asking to check understanding, staying on topic, and linking to Maya's remarks? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Does the white fur help them hide from predators in the snow?
answer_options.B: Why do you think having small ears helps the fox stay warm?
answer_options.C: Have you ever seen a desert fox at the zoo?
answer_options.D: What kinds of food do Arctic foxes hunt in the winter?

answer_explanation: Options A and B are correct because they ask questions that check understanding of the specific information Maya presented (fur color change and ear size), stay on the topic of Arctic fox adaptations, and directly link to her remarks. Option C is incorrect because it shifts the topic to desert foxes, which is off-topic and does not link to Maya's remarks about Arctic adaptations. Option D is incorrect because while it mentions Arctic foxes, it does not check understanding of the information Maya presented about fur and ears; instead, it asks about diet, which introduces a new subtopic unrelated to her remarks.
subject: ELA

question:
During a class meeting, students discuss ways to help the environment. Maya says, "I think we should start a paper recycling program in our classroom because we use a lot of notebook paper every day."

Which responses show students explaining their own ideas and understanding in light of Maya's suggestion? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: I agree with Maya. We could also collect plastic bottles and turn them into planters for the garden.
answer_options.B: I agree that recycling paper is a good idea for our classroom.
answer_options.C: Maya's idea about recycling paper could work alongside a compost bin for lunch scraps, since both help reduce school waste.
answer_options.D: I helped my family sort recyclables at home last weekend.

answer_explanation: Options A and C are correct because both students reference Maya's suggestion and then explain their own related ideas—adding a bottle collection program in A and proposing a complementary compost system in C—thereby demonstrating their own understanding developed through the discussion. Option B is incorrect because it offers only simple agreement without explaining any personal idea or building upon Maya's suggestion. Option D is incorrect because it shares a personal story that is unrelated to the classroom discussion and does not respond to Maya's specific proposal.
subject: ELA

question:
During a class discussion about the story "The Tall Tree," Maya says, "I think Max was brave to climb so high because he wasn't thinking about the danger—he only cared about saving the kitten."

Leo wants to explain his own ideas and understanding in light of Maya's comment. Which of the following statements show Leo explaining his own ideas in light of the discussion? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: I think Maya makes a good point about bravery, but I see Max's action as showing responsibility instead—he knew the kitten was helpless and that mattered more than his own safety.
answer_options.B: Maya pointed out that Max was brave because he didn't think about the danger when he climbed the tall tree to save the kitten.
answer_options.C: I completely agree with Maya that Max was brave and did not consider the danger when he rescued the kitten from the tall tree.
answer_options.D: I understand Maya's view about bravery, but I also noticed that Max looked down and his hands shook, which suggests he knew the risk yet chose to help anyway.

answer_explanation: Options A and D are correct because both statements explicitly reference Maya's comment about bravery and then explain Leo's own interpretation or understanding of Max's actions, demonstrating how to explain ideas 'in light of the discussion.' Option B is incorrect because it merely summarizes Maya's point without adding Leo's own ideas or perspective. Option C is incorrect because it only agrees with Maya's interpretation without explaining Leo's own understanding or adding new insight to the discussion.
subject: ELA

question:
During a class discussion about a story, Maya says, "I think Jayla made a good choice when she helped her elderly neighbor carry groceries because she cared more about helping someone than about being on time for soccer practice."

Which responses show a student explaining their own ideas and understanding in light of what Maya said?

Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: I agree with Maya, but I also think Jayla felt worried about disappointing her coach, which makes her choice even harder.
answer_options.B: I agree with Maya that Jayla made a good choice because neighbors should always help each other.
answer_options.C: I see Maya's point about caring, yet I believe Jayla should have called her coach first to explain her delay.
answer_options.D: Maya and I both noticed that Jayla carried three bags of groceries up the hill to the neighbor's house.

answer_explanation: Options A and C are correct. Option A explains the student's own idea by agreeing with Maya and adding a new thought about Jayla's internal worry, showing deeper understanding of the character's conflict in light of Maya's point about priorities. Option C explains the student's own idea by acknowledging Maya's view but offering a different perspective on what Jayla should have done, demonstrating how the student has developed their own reasoning in response to Maya's comment. Option B is incorrect because it simply agrees with Maya's conclusion without explaining the student's own ideas or building upon the specific discussion about caring versus being on time. Option D is incorrect because it merely summarizes a detail from the story without explaining any interpretation of the character's choice or connecting to Maya's point.
subject: ELA

question:
During science time, the class listened to a recording about how chocolate is made. The speaker explained that chocolate comes from cacao beans that grow on trees in warm climates. First, the beans are dried and roasted. Then they are ground into a liquid that is mixed with sugar to make chocolate.

Which statements describe information from the recording? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Cacao beans grow on trees in warm places.
answer_options.B: The beans are roasted and then ground into liquid.
answer_options.C: Chocolate is made by freezing milk and sugar.
answer_options.D: Cacao trees grow best in cold, snowy weather.

answer_explanation: Options A and B are correct because they accurately reflect details stated in the recording: the speaker said cacao beans grow on trees in warm climates, and that the beans are roasted and ground into liquid. Option C is incorrect because freezing milk was not mentioned; the recording described grinding beans into liquid and mixing it with sugar. Option D is incorrect because the recording specified warm climates, not cold, snowy weather.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the transcript of a science documentary about weather patterns.

"Welcome to Earth Science Today. We're exploring how weather patterns shape our planet. First, warm air masses hold more moisture than cold air masses, which is why humid regions near the equator receive heavy rainfall throughout the year. When this warm, moist air collides with cold air fronts at higher latitudes, the temperature difference forces the warm air to rise rapidly, cool down, and release its moisture as intense storms. Additionally, mountains act as barriers that force air upward, causing precipitation on one side while creating dry shadows on the other. The presentation showed data from the Himalayas, where the southern slopes receive over 400 inches of rain annually while the northern side remains a desert."

Which statements express the **main ideas** of the presentation? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Weather patterns are created when warm and cold air masses meet and interact.
answer_options.B: Warm air can hold more water vapor than cold air can contain.
answer_options.C: Mountain barriers force air upward, causing rain on one side and dry conditions on the other.
answer_options.D: The Himalayas receive over 400 inches of rain on their southern slopes but remain dry on the northern side.

answer_explanation: Options A and C are correct because they state the broad, central concepts that organize the presentation: the interaction of air masses and the effect of mountains on weather patterns. These statements cover entire sections of the talk. Option B is incorrect because it explains a specific scientific property (warm air capacity) that supports the main idea about air mass interactions, but it is too narrow to be a main idea itself. Option D is incorrect because it cites a specific example (the Himalayas) that illustrates the mountain barrier concept, making it a supporting detail rather than a main idea.
subject: ELA

question:
A teacher played a short educational video about clouds and read the following transcript aloud to the class:

"Clouds are made of tiny water droplets or ice crystals. When warm air rises high into the sky, it cools down and the water vapor in the air changes into liquid droplets. These droplets stick together to form clouds. Different types of clouds bring different kinds of weather. Dark, gray clouds often mean rain is coming, while fluffy white clouds usually mean fair weather."

Which statements correctly identify the main idea or supporting details from the transcript? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Clouds are created when water vapor turns into liquid droplets in cool air.
answer_options.B: All clouds bring rain to the earth.
answer_options.C: Clouds are made of tiny water droplets or ice crystals.
answer_options.D: Warm air rises and cools down to help form clouds.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the transcript states that when air cools, water vapor changes into liquid droplets that form clouds. Option C is correct because the transcript explicitly states that clouds are made of tiny water droplets or ice crystals. Option D is correct because the transcript describes the process of warm air rising, cooling down, and forming cloud droplets. Option B is incorrect because the transcript states that different clouds bring different weather, specifically noting that fluffy white clouds mean fair weather rather than rain.
subject: ELA

question:
A park ranger is speaking to a class about how bees make honey. She says, "Honeybees collect nectar from flowers using their long tongues. They store the nectar in a special stomach and take it back to the hive, where other bees help turn it into honey."

A student answers the question, "How do bees make honey?" based on the ranger's explanation. Which responses include appropriate elaboration and detail? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Bees use their long tongues to drink nectar from flowers.
answer_options.B: Bees make honey by mixing nectar with water in their stomachs.
answer_options.C: Bees bring the nectar back to the hive where other bees help change it into honey.
answer_options.D: Bees use their wings to fan the honey until it becomes thick.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it includes the specific detail from the speaker about bees using their long tongues to collect nectar. Option C is correct because it elaborates on the process by mentioning that bees return to the hive and other bees help transform the nectar into honey. Option B is incorrect because the speaker stated that bees store nectar in a special stomach, not that they mix it with water. Option D is incorrect because the speaker did not mention bees using their wings to fan the honey.
subject: ELA

question:
Listen to the short talk about how honeybees communicate.

"Honeybees use a special dance to tell other bees where to find food. When a bee finds flowers, it flies back to the hive and does a waggle dance. The direction of the dance shows which way to fly, and how long the bee waggles tells the distance. However, if the food is very close to the hive, the bee does a simpler round dance instead."

Which responses appropriately ask for elaboration or provide detailed information about the speaker's points? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: How does the length of the waggle dance tell other bees how far away the flowers are?
answer_options.B: The round dance is used for nearby food, while the waggle dance shows both direction and distance for food that is farther away.
answer_options.C: Bees do the waggle dance because they are excited and want to celebrate finding nectar.
answer_options.D: Bees fly back to the hive to tell the other bees about the flowers.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it asks a specific follow-up question that seeks elaboration on the detail about how the duration of the waggle indicates distance. Option B is correct because it provides a detailed answer that appropriately elaborates on the speaker's points by synthesizing the specific purposes of both dances mentioned. Option C is incorrect because it introduces an explanation (bees feeling excited) that was not mentioned by the speaker and contradicts the stated purpose of communicating location. Option D is incorrect because, while factually true, it lacks the appropriate elaboration and specific detail about the dance types and their meanings, offering only a vague summary.
subject: ELA

question:
Listen to the short talk.

"Honeybees are important pollinators for many plants. When a bee lands on a flower to collect nectar, tiny grains of pollen stick to its body. The bee then carries this pollen to the next flower, which helps the plant make fruit and seeds."

Which responses use details from the speaker to explain why honeybees are important? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: Bees are important because they make honey for people to eat.
answer_options.B: Bees carry pollen on their bodies from one flower to another.
answer_options.C: Pollen helps plants create fruit and seeds.
answer_options.D: Bees fly quickly between many flowers every day.

answer_explanation: Options B and C are correct because they include specific elaboration and details provided by the speaker. Option B accurately describes the process of pollen sticking to the bee's body and being carried between flowers, which the speaker mentioned. Option C correctly identifies that pollen helps plants make fruit and seeds, using the speaker's specific detail. Option A is incorrect because, although bees do make honey, the speaker did not mention honey production in this talk. Option D is incorrect because the speaker did not describe the speed at which bees fly or how many flowers they visit daily.
subject: ELA

question: Maya is giving a report about dolphins to her class. Which actions should Maya take to follow the guidelines for effective speaking? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Speaking clearly and at a pace that is not too fast or too slow.
answer_options.B: Mentioning that dolphins are mammals without giving any examples or descriptions.
answer_options.C: Including descriptive details about the size, color, and eating habits of dolphins.
answer_options.D: Talking about her family vacation to the beach last summer.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the standard requires speaking clearly at an understandable pace, and using a moderate speed helps ensure the audience can follow along. Option C is correct because the standard requires including relevant, descriptive details when reporting on a topic, and describing the physical characteristics and behaviors of dolphins provides such details. Option B is incorrect because while it states an accurate fact, it explicitly lacks the descriptive details required to fully meet the standard. Option D is incorrect because discussing a personal vacation is not relevant to the topic of dolphins, failing the requirement for relevant details.
subject: ELA

question: Maya is preparing an oral report about monarch butterflies. She wants to include sections that demonstrate appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details. Which of the following excerpts from her draft meet these criteria? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Every fall, millions of monarch butterflies travel up to 3,000 miles from Canada to Mexico. Their delicate orange and black wings beat steadily as they soar over mountains and fields to reach their warm winter home.
answer_options.B: Monarchs are orange and black. They fly south in the winter to stay warm.
answer_options.C: Female monarchs lay tiny white eggs on milkweed plants. The caterpillars that hatch have bold stripes of black, yellow, and white as they chew through the green leaves.
answer_options.D: Painted lady butterflies travel long distances and have orange wings with black and white spots on the tips.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it provides specific, appropriate facts (3,000 miles, Canada to Mexico) and relevant descriptive details (delicate orange and black wings, beat steadily, soar over mountains). Option C is correct because it provides specific facts about the life cycle (eggs on milkweed, caterpillars hatch) and vivid descriptive details (tiny white eggs, bold stripes of black, yellow, and white, chew through green leaves). Option B is incorrect because, while the facts are accurate, the description lacks specific, vivid details required by the standard (it merely states colors and direction without descriptive language). Option D is incorrect because it provides facts about painted lady butterflies rather than monarchs, making the information inappropriate for a report specifically about monarchs.
subject: ELA

question: Emma is giving a report to her class about how honeybees make honey. She wants to include appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details to help her classmates understand the process. Which sentences should Emma include in her report? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Honeybees visit thousands of colorful flowers to collect sweet nectar, which they store in a special honey stomach called a crop.
answer_options.B: I think honeybees are the most amazing insects in the entire world.
answer_options.C: Inside the hive, worker bees pass the nectar to each other and fan it with their wings until it becomes thick and golden.
answer_options.D: Honeybees make honey to feed their colony during the cold winter months.

answer_explanation: Options A and C are correct because they provide specific facts about the honey-making process along with relevant, descriptive details (such as 'colorful flowers,' 'sweet nectar,' 'special honey stomach,' 'fan it with their wings,' and 'thick and golden') that help the audience visualize how honey is made. Option B is incorrect because it expresses a personal opinion rather than an objective fact. Option D is incorrect because, although it states a true fact about why bees make honey, it lacks the descriptive details about the actual process of making honey that would help the audience understand and visualize the steps involved.
subject: ELA

question: Maya is planning an audio recording of a poem about the ocean. She wants to make sure her presentation follows the guidelines for good recordings. Which of the following plans would help Maya create a successful recording? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Practice reading aloud at a steady pace so listeners can understand every word
answer_options.B: Hold up drawings of ocean waves and fish while reading the lines that describe them
answer_options.C: Read the poem as quickly as possible to fit it into one minute
answer_options.D: Display photographs of mountains and forests during the recording

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because practicing reading at a steady pace ensures the recording is fluid and easy to understand, which aligns with the standard's requirement for understandable pacing. Option B is correct because showing drawings of ocean waves and fish during matching lines adds visual displays that enhance the specific details about the ocean described in the poem. Option C is incorrect because reading as quickly as possible makes the recording difficult to understand and prevents fluid reading. Option D is incorrect because photographs of mountains and forests do not relate to the ocean poem and therefore do not enhance or emphasize the facts and details being presented.
subject: ELA

question: Sasha is recording her presentation about the life cycle of a frog. She wants to create an engaging audio recording that demonstrates fluid reading at an understandable pace and to add visual displays that enhance the facts she describes. Which of the following strategies should Sasha use to meet these goals? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Practice reading the section about the tadpole's tail shrinking several times before recording to ensure the words flow smoothly without hesitation.
answer_options.B: Display a photograph of an adult frog while reading the description of the tiny eggs floating in the water.
answer_options.C: Read the exciting conclusion about the frog jumping onto land at a slightly faster pace than the rest of the story, while speaking clearly.
answer_options.D: Show a series of unlabeled pictures of different pond animals in random order throughout the recording.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because rehearsing specific sections ensures fluid reading, allowing Sasha to read smoothly without stumbling, which demonstrates the 'fluid reading' required by the standard. Option C is correct because adjusting the reading pace to match the excitement of the content makes the recording engaging while maintaining an understandable pace, rather than reading in a monotone or rushing through the entire text. Option B is incorrect because displaying an image of an adult frog while describing the egg stage does not enhance the specific facts being presented at that moment; the visual should match the particular detail being described. Option D is incorrect because showing unlabeled pictures of various animals in random order does not emphasize or enhance the specific facts about the frog's life cycle and may distract from the information being shared.
subject: ELA

question: Sofia is recording a story about a volcanic eruption for her class. She wants to make sure her audio is engaging and easy to understand, and she plans to add visual displays. Which of her plans will help her meet these goals? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: Reading the eruption scene with a powerful, clear voice and slowing down to emphasize important safety steps
answer_options.B: Displaying a labeled diagram of a volcano's layers when the recording describes how magma moves underground
answer_options.C: Using several unlabeled photos of different mountains arranged in random order on her slide
answer_options.D: Speaking in a quiet monotone throughout the entire recording to maintain a steady volume

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because using a clear voice with varied speed and emphasis demonstrates fluid reading at an understandable pace, making the recording engaging. Option B is correct because showing a labeled diagram at the moment the related fact is described adds a visual display that emphasizes and enhances the specific detail. Option C is incorrect because unlabeled pictures in random order do not effectively emphasize or clarify the facts in the story. Option D is incorrect because speaking in a quiet monotone without variation fails to demonstrate fluid, expressive reading and is less likely to engage the audience.
subject: ELA

question: During a science discussion, the teacher asks, "What happened to the ice cube when we left it on the counter?" Select ALL responses that use complete sentences to provide appropriate detail or clarification.

answer_options.A: It melted into a puddle of water.
answer_options.B: Turned into water.
answer_options.C: The ice cube became liquid because the room was warm.
answer_options.D: Because it was hot in the room.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it is a complete sentence with a subject ('It') and a predicate ('melted into a puddle of water') that provides specific detail about what happened. Option C is correct because it is a complete sentence with a subject ('The ice cube') and a predicate ('became liquid...') that offers clarification with specific information about the change and the reason. Option B is incorrect because it is a sentence fragment; it is missing a subject (such as 'It' or 'The ice'). Option D is incorrect because it is a dependent clause starting with 'Because' and does not express a complete thought; it leaves the reader waiting for the rest of the sentence (e.g., what happened because it was hot?).
subject: ELA

question: During a science discussion, Ms. Rivera asks Carlos to clarify his group's observation about the plant growth experiment. She specifically requests that he speak in complete sentences and provide measurable details about the changes observed. Which of Carlos's responses meet these requirements? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: We observed that the seedling near the window grew exactly four centimeters in one week, while the one in the dark cabinet grew only one centimeter.
answer_options.B: Our data shows the plant in sunlight measured six centimeters tall on Friday, whereas the shade plant remained at two centimeters.
answer_options.C: Checking the heights every day and noticing the sunny one was bigger.
answer_options.D: The plant grew really fast and got much taller than the other one and the leaves were bigger too.

answer_explanation: Options A and B are correct because they are grammatically complete sentences that provide specific, measurable details (exact heights in centimeters and time periods) as requested by the teacher. Option C is incorrect because it is a sentence fragment; it lacks a subject performing the action and a finite verb, making it an incomplete thought. Option D is incorrect because it is a run-on sentence that joins multiple independent thoughts without proper punctuation or conjunctions, and it uses vague language ('really fast,' 'much taller') instead of the specific measurable details required.
subject: ELA

question: While presenting their group science project, the teacher asks, "Can you clarify how much the seedling grew? Use a complete sentence with a specific measurement." Which responses meet these requirements? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: Our seedling grew exactly 3 centimeters taller over the two weeks.
answer_options.B: It grew 3 cm because we watered it every day and gave it sunlight.
answer_options.C: The plant grew a lot over the two weeks.
answer_options.D: About 3 centimeters.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it uses a complete sentence (subject 'Our seedling' and predicate 'grew') and provides the requested specific measurement (3 centimeters) to clarify the growth. Option B is correct because it is a complete sentence (subject 'It' and predicate 'grew') and provides the specific measurement (3 cm) along with clarifying details. Option C is incorrect because, although it is a complete sentence, it fails to provide the requested specific measurable detail; 'a lot' is vague and does not clarify exactly how much the plant grew. Option D is incorrect because it is a sentence fragment lacking both a subject and a verb, and therefore does not meet the requirement to use a complete sentence.
subject: ELA

question: Read these sentences from student opinion papers. Which ones introduce the topic, state an opinion, and list supporting reasons? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: I believe that children should help with chores at home because it teaches responsibility and helps the family work together.
answer_options.B: In my opinion, libraries are the best places to visit because they are quiet, full of books, and free for everyone.
answer_options.C: We should have more art classes in school so students can be creative, learn about colors, and express their feelings.
answer_options.D: I think pizza is good.

answer_explanation: Options A, B, and C correctly introduce a specific topic (chores, libraries, art classes), state a clear opinion using phrases such as 'I believe,' 'In my opinion,' and 'We should,' and create an organizational structure that lists reasons using connecting words like 'because' and 'so.'
subject: ELA

question: Maya is writing an opinion essay about why her school should start later in the morning. She needs an introduction that introduces the topic, clearly states her opinion, and lists the specific reasons she will discuss in her essay. Which of the following sentences could Maya use as her introduction? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: I believe school should start later because students need more sleep, breakfast time is too rushed, and the morning traffic is dangerous for walkers.
answer_options.B: Our school day should begin at nine o'clock since teenagers are naturally sleepy early in the morning, many students skip breakfast when they wake up late, and bus rides are safer when the sun is up.
answer_options.C: In my opinion, starting school later would benefit students for many important reasons.
answer_options.D: Early school start times often make students feel tired and cause them to rush through breakfast.
